disclosures.title: RealTek HD Audio Codec Jack Retasking
disclosures.summary: CWE-862 (Missing Authorization) — 9-year-old unfiled vulnerability found through TARA systematic gap analysis.

disclosures.title: Lab Streaming Layer (LSL) Transport Protocol
disclosures.summary: Transport and discovery protocol vulnerabilities affecting confidentiality and integrity of streamed neural data. Full report with CWE mappings, CVSS v4.0 scoring, and working PoC submitted via responsible disclosure. LSL team acknowledged and is building Secure LSL (https://eeglab.org/secureLSL/) with end-to-end encryption, device authentication, replay protection, and session key rotation. Mitigation: upgrade to Secure LSL when available.

standards.title: NISS v1.0
standards.summary: Formally reviewed by the FIRST.org CVSS SIG in a SIG meeting. The SIG acknowledged the proposal but currently lacks subject-matter experts in neurotechnology to evaluate implementation. FIRST.org offered to add NISS as a resource in their GitHub repo sources. Kevin was accepted as a member of the CVSS SIG committee and is actively looking for SMEs who can help implement the extension correctly across international standards.

specifications.title: QIF Whitepaper
specifications.summary: Proposed open security framework for brain-computer interfaces — hourglass architecture, threat modeling, neural impact scoring.

specifications.title: NSP Protocol
specifications.summary: Post-quantum neural security protocol. Five defense layers at 3.25% implant power budget.

specifications.title: NISS Scoring System
specifications.summary: CVSS v4.0 extension for neural interfaces. Six metrics: Biological Impact, Cognitive Reconnaissance, Cognitive Disruption, Consent Violation, Reversibility, Neuroplasticity.

timeline.title: QIF Framework v1.0 Published
timeline.description: 71 attack techniques across 11 tactics and 7 domains. Full TARA Atlas with technique IDs.

timeline.title: NSP Protocol Specification Released
timeline.description: Post-quantum neural security protocol. Under 4% implant power budget. Wire-level encryption for BCI data streams.

timeline.title: TARA Atlas Published
timeline.description: Dual-use atlas mapping 71 attack techniques to therapeutic analogs. 60% of attack mechanisms have clinical counterparts.

timeline.title: NISS v1.1 Specification Complete
timeline.description: First CVSS v4.0 extension for neural interfaces. Six metrics: Biological Impact, Cognitive Reconnaissance, Cognitive Disruption, Consent Violation, Reversibility, Neuroplasticity.

timeline.title: 71 CVSS v4.0 Base Vector Mappings Complete
timeline.description: Every technique in the TARA Atlas mapped to a CVSS v4.0 base vector with companion NISS extension vector.

timeline.title: First BCI Software PoC Exploit
timeline.description: Three-phase exploit chain against a widely-used BCI data streaming library. Responsible disclosure in progress.

timeline.title: NISS Submitted to FIRST.org CVSS SIG
timeline.description: Formal proposal sent to cvss@first.org for NISS registration as a CVSS v4.0 extension under Section 3.11 of the User Guide.

timeline.title: First CVE Filed via TARA Framework
timeline.description: CWE-862 (Missing Authorization) on RealTek HD Audio codec jack retasking. 9-year-old unfiled vulnerability found through systematic gap analysis. Disclosed to TWCERT/CC, RealTek, and MITRE.

timeline.title: Consumer Sensor Exploitation Domain Added
timeline.description: New 8th domain (S) with 4 tactics and 8 initial techniques covering upstream consumer device attacks. Later expanded to 28 S-domain techniques (99 total).

timeline.title: S-Domain Expanded to 28 Techniques (T0080–T0099)
timeline.description: 20 new consumer sensor techniques across audio, optical, IMU, RF, thermal, and biometric categories. 5 chain techniques document multi-stage escalation paths. Registry reaches 99 techniques.

timeline.title: DSM-5-TR Neural Impact Chain Mapping
timeline.description: Research mapping from BCI security severity scores to psychiatric diagnostic categories. All techniques mapped to DSM-5-TR diagnostic clusters via the Neural Impact Chain. Requires clinical validation. See the research proposal v6 or TARA changelog for details.

timeline.title: Registry Crosses 100 Techniques
timeline.description: 3 new consumer device side-channel techniques (T0100–T0102): neural steganographic encoding, multi-modal keystroke inference, and screen photometry sensing. WiFi CSI consumer-grade capabilities merged into T0090. All confirmed dual-use with clinical applications.
